title: Aerangis citrata
intro: Aerangis citrata is a species of orchid native to Africa, known for its unique and fragrant flowers. The health benefits of Aerangis citrata are attributed to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Aerangis citrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aerangis citrata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support respiratory health and immune function.
taxonomy: Aerangis citrata is a species of orchid, classified within the genus Aerangis and the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of epiphytic orchid, native to tropical Africa. The genus Aerangis is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. Aerangis citrata is characterized by its distinctive yellow-green flowers and slender growth habit. This classification reflects the orchid's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.
